"Let us examine the data presented on the board with great care.

midnight brings a quiet moment to think about the sum of all values.

The five largest entries make up a significant portion of the total amount.

The five smallest entries also contribute a fixed fraction to the whole.

Adding these two groups together gives us a combined value for ten numbers.

This sum represents fifty-five percent of the entire collection of numbers.

We must now determine how many items remain outside these two groups.

If the remaining part equals forty-five percent, we can find the total count.

The final answer is that there are twenty numbers written on the board."
